warning: AI-assisted monitoring. Verify against official legislation before product approval, export, label declaration, or customer compliance confirmation.

rules.sourceTitle: Food Additives DB (GSFA Online) | CODEXALIMENTARIUS FAO-WHO
rules.notes: The Codex General Standard for Food Additives (GSFA, Codex STAN 192-1995) sets forth the conditions for permitted food additive use in all foods. Specific provisions, including limits, are available in the online database and must be verified by food additive, functional class, and food category.
rules.evidenceQuote: The "Codex General Standard for Food Additives" (GSFA, Codex STAN 192-1995) sets forth the conditions under which permitted food additives may be used in all foods

rules.sourceTitle: Contaminants | CODEXALIMENTARIUS FAO-WHO
rules.notes: A Code of Practice (CXC 78-2017) exists for the prevention and reduction of mycotoxins in spices. Compliance with this code is required to manage mycotoxin contamination.
rules.evidenceQuote: CXC 78-2017 Code of Practice for the Prevention and Reduction of Mycotoxins in Spices CCCF 2017

rules.parameter: Specific contaminants (e.g., mycotoxins, metals like arsenic, lead, mercury)
rules.sourceTitle: Contaminants | CODEXALIMENTARIUS FAO-WHO
rules.notes: The Codex Alimentarius Commission establishes maximum levels (MLs) for contaminants and naturally occurring toxicants in food and feed. Specific MLs for various contaminants are detailed in the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995) and must be verified.
rules.evidenceQuote: The Codex maximum level (ML) for a contaminant in a food or feed commodity is the maximum concentration of that substance recommended by the Codex Alimentarius Commission to be legally permitted in that commodity.

rules.sourceTitle: Database - Food Safety - European Commission
rules.notes: Verification of approved food additives and their conditions of use for seasonings and snack products is required by consulting the EU Additives Database, which is based on Annex II of Regulation (EC) No 1333/2008.
rules.evidenceQuote: This database can serve as a tool to inform about the food additives approved for use in food in the EU and their conditions of use. It is based on the Union list of food additives. This list is in Annex II of Regulation (EC) No 1333/2008.

rules.sourceTitle: Microbiological criteria - Food Safety - European Commission
rules.notes: Verification of food safety criteria for relevant foodborne bacteria, their toxins and metabolites (e.g., Salmonella, Listeria monocytogenes, Enterobacter sakazakii, staphylococcal enterotoxins, histamine) in specific foods, including seasonings and snack products, is required by consulting Commission Regulation (EC) No 2073/2005.
rules.evidenceQuote: Commission Regulation (EC) No 2073/2005 on microbiological criteria for foods, applicable from 1 January 2006, lays down food safety criteria for relevant foodborne bacteria, their toxins and metabolites, such as Salmonella , Listeria monocytogenes , Enterobacter sakazakii , staphylococcal enterotoxins and histamine in specific foods.

rules.sourceTitle: Labeling of prepackaged food stuffs
rules.notes: Verification of specific labelling requirements is required by consulting the full regulation.
rules.evidenceQuote: GSO 9:2022 لائحة فنية خليجية --> بطاقات المواد الغذائية المعبأة

rules.sourceTitle: Microbiological Criteria for Foodstuffs
rules.notes: Verification of specific microbiological limits is required by consulting the full regulation.
rules.evidenceQuote: This GSO technical regulation is concerned with microbiological limits for some foodstuffs intended for human consumption and for some food ingredients used in food industry.

rules.parameter: Permitted food additives and their maximum levels
rules.sourceTitle: Compendium of Food Additives Regulations
rules.notes: Specific permitted food additives and their maximum levels for seasoning, snack food, and ready-to-eat snacks must be verified against the detailed schedules within this compendium.
rules.evidenceQuote: Compendium_Food_Additives_Regulations

rules.sourceTitle: Compendium of Labelling and Display Regulations
rules.notes: Specific mandatory allergen declarations for seasoning, snack food, and ready-to-eat snacks must be verified against this compendium.
rules.evidenceQuote: Compendium_Labelling_Display

rules.sourceTitle: Compendium of Labelling and Display Regulations
rules.notes: All mandatory labelling information for seasoning, snack food, and ready-to-eat snacks must be verified against this compendium.
rules.evidenceQuote: Compendium_Labelling_Display

rules.limit: Prohibited unless specified as unlikely to cause harm by MHLW
rules.effectiveDate: Act No. 233 of 1947 (Last Version: Act No. 46 of 2018)
rules.sourceTitle: Food Sanitation Act
rules.notes: Additives (excluding natural flavoring agents and traditional foods used as additives) and preparations/food containing additives are prohibited from sale, production, import, processing, use, storage, or display for sale, unless specified as safe by MHLW.
rules.evidenceQuote: Additives (excluding natural flavoring agents and things that have generally been served for human consumption, and are used as additives)... must not be sold... except for cases that the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare specifies as unlikely to cause harm to human health

rules.limit: Prohibited if likely to cause harm due to contamination or foreign substances
rules.effectiveDate: Act No. 233 of 1947 (Last Version: Act No. 46 of 2018)
rules.sourceTitle: Food Sanitation Act
rules.notes: Food or additives likely to cause harm to human health due to contamination or the addition of foreign substances or on other grounds are prohibited.
rules.evidenceQuote: those which are likely to cause harm to human health due to contamination or the addition of foreign substances or on other grounds.

rules.parameter: Residues of agricultural chemicals, feed additives, and pharmaceuticals
rules.effectiveDate: Act No. 233 of 1947 (Last Version: Act No. 46 of 2018)
rules.sourceTitle: Food Sanitation Act
rules.notes: Food containing residues of agricultural chemicals, feed additives, or pharmaceuticals exceeding the quantity specified by MHLW as unlikely to cause harm is prohibited. This forms the basis of the Positive List System for Agricultural Chemical Residues.
rules.evidenceQuote: Food in which substances that are the ingredients of agricultural chemicals... feed additives... and pharmaceutical products... remain in a quantity exceeding the quantity that the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare specifies as unlikely to cause harm to human health... must not be produced, imported... or sold

rules.limit: Prohibited if containing or bearing toxic or harmful substances
rules.effectiveDate: Act No. 233 of 1947 (Last Version: Act No. 46 of 2018)
rules.sourceTitle: Food Sanitation Act
rules.notes: Food or additives containing or suspected to contain toxic or harmful substances are prohibited, unless the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare specifies they are unlikely to cause harm.
rules.evidenceQuote: those which contain or bear toxic or harmful substances or are suspected to contain or bear such substances; provided, however, that this does not apply when the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare specifies that the substances are unlikely to cause harm to human health

rules.parameter: Conformance to MHLW established standards and criteria
rules.limit: Verification required for specific criteria and standards
rules.effectiveDate: Act No. 233 of 1947 (Last Version: Act No. 46 of 2018)
rules.sourceTitle: Food Sanitation Act
rules.notes: The Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare may establish criteria for methods of producing, processing, using, cooking, or preserving food/additives, and standards for their ingredients. These must be verified.
rules.evidenceQuote: the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare may establish the criteria for the methods of producing, processing, using, cooking, or preserving food or additives... or may establish standards for the ingredients of food or additives

rules.parameter: Food business operator responsibility for safety and traceability
rules.limit: Endeavor to ensure safety, obtain knowledge/skills, ensure raw material safety, conduct voluntary inspections, record necessary information (e.g., seller name), and take prompt measures for hazards.
rules.effectiveDate: Act No. 233 of 1947 (Last Version: Act No. 46 of 2018)
rules.sourceTitle: Food Sanitation Act
rules.notes: Food business operators have a responsibility to endeavor to ensure the safety of their products, maintain records for traceability, and take appropriate actions in case of hazards.
rules.evidenceQuote: A food business operator... must endeavor to ensure the safety of the food, additives... and for that purpose, they must endeavor to obtain the knowledge and skills necessary... ensure the safety of raw materials... conduct voluntary inspections... make a record of any necessary information... and take any necessary measures appropriately and promptly

rules.parameter: Non-conformance to MHLW established standards and criteria
rules.limit: Prohibited if non-conforming to MHLW standards or criteria
rules.effectiveDate: Act No. 233 of 1947 (Last Version: Act No. 46 of 2018)
rules.sourceTitle: Food Sanitation Act
rules.notes: Food or additives that do not conform to MHLW established standards or criteria are prohibited from production, import, processing, use, cooking, preservation, or sale.
rules.evidenceQuote: food or additives that do not conform to such standards must not be produced, imported, processed, used, cooked, preserved, or sold.

rules.effectiveDate: Act No. 233 of 1947 (Last Version: Act No. 46 of 2018)
rules.sourceTitle: Food Sanitation Act
rules.notes: Food and additives specified by MHLW Order as requiring important process control measures must be imported only if produced/processed in MHLW-specified locations that take those measures.
rules.evidenceQuote: The food and additives specified by Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare Order as requiring measures to control particularly important processes... must not be imported... unless they have been produced or processed in the countries or regions, or facilities specified by the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare

rules.limit: Verification required for certificates from exporting country's governmental organization
rules.effectiveDate: Act No. 233 of 1947 (Last Version: Act No. 46 of 2018)
rules.sourceTitle: Food Sanitation Act
rules.notes: Food and additives specified by MHLW Order as requiring certification of food sanitation management at the production site must not be imported without attached certificates from the exporting country's governmental organization.
rules.evidenceQuote: The food and additives specified by Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare Order as requiring certification of the conditions of food sanitation management at the production site... must not be imported... unless they have certificates or copies thereof attached which have been issued by a governmental organization of an exporting country

rules.parameter: Diseases or abnormalities in source animals
rules.limit: Prohibited for sale/use as food if from diseased/abnormal livestock/poultry
rules.effectiveDate: Act No. 233 of 1947 (Last Version: Act No. 46 of 2018)
rules.sourceTitle: Food Sanitation Act
rules.notes: Meat, bones, milk, organs, and blood of livestock or poultry with specified diseases/abnormalities or that have died are prohibited, unless specified by MHLW Order or found safe. Imported products require certificates of sanitation.
rules.evidenceQuote: The meat, bones, milk, organs, and blood of livestock... which have or are suspected to have any of the diseases listed in item (i) or item (iii), have any of the abnormalities listed in item (i) or item (iii), or have died... must not be sold as food

rules.effectiveDate: Act No. 233 of 1947 (Last Version: Act No. 46 of 2018)
rules.sourceTitle: Food Sanitation Act
rules.notes: Applies to collection, production, processing, use, cooking, storage, transport, display, and delivery of food or additives meant for sale.
rules.evidenceQuote: Food or additives which are meant to be sold... must be collected, produced, processed, used, cooked, stored, transported, displayed and delivered in a clean and sanitary manner.

rules.limit: Prohibited if contaminated with pathogens likely to cause harm
rules.effectiveDate: Act No. 233 of 1947 (Last Version: Act No. 46 of 2018)
rules.sourceTitle: Food Sanitation Act
rules.notes: Food or additives contaminated with pathogens or suspected to be so, and which are likely to cause harm to human health, are prohibited.
rules.evidenceQuote: those which are contaminated with pathogens or are suspected to be so, and which are likely to cause harm to human health

rules.limit: Prohibited if rotten, deteriorated, or immature (unless proven safe)
rules.effectiveDate: Act No. 233 of 1947 (Last Version: Act No. 46 of 2018)
rules.sourceTitle: Food Sanitation Act
rules.notes: Food or additives that are rotten, deteriorated, or immature are prohibited from sale, collection, production, import, processing, use, cooking, storage, or display for sale, unless unlikely to cause harm and recognized as edible.
rules.evidenceQuote: those which are rotten or have deteriorated, or are immature; provided, however, that this does not apply to those which are unlikely to cause harm to human health in general and are recognized as edible

rules.foodCategory: Prepackaged foodstuffs, including seasoning, snack food, and processed foods

rules.notes: This technical regulation specifies the general requirements for labels of prepackaged foodstuffs. Specific details must be verified by consulting the full standard.
rules.evidenceQuote: بطاقات المواد الغذائية المعبأة

rules.foodCategory: Halal food products, including seasoning, snack food, and processed foods intended to be Halal

rules.foodCategory: Unprocessed genetically modified agricultural products used as raw materials for processed foods, seasoning, and snack products

rules.notes: This technical regulation specifies general requirements for unprocessed genetically modified agricultural products, which may impact ingredients used in seasoning, snack, and processed food products. Specific details must be verified by consulting the full standard.

rules.foodCategory: Food products, including seasoning, snack food, and processed foods

rules.notes: This technical regulation specifies mandatory shelf life periods for various food products. Specific periods must be verified by consulting the full standard.

rules.foodCategory: Food products, including seasoning, snack food, and processed foods
rules.sourceTitle: GSO 150-2:2013 مواصفة قياسية خليجية --> فترات صلاحية المنتجات الغذائية - الجزء الثاني : فترات الصلاحية الاختيارية
rules.notes: This standard provides guidelines for optional shelf life periods for food products. Specific details must be verified by consulting the full standard.

rules.foodCategory: seasoning powder, seasoning sauce, snack seasoning, ready-to-eat snacks
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Food Additives
rules.notes: If two or more Class II chemical preservatives (e.g., sorbic acid or benzoic acid) are used, the Calculation Tool for Class II Chemical Preservatives must be used to calculate permitted levels.
rules.evidenceQuote: If two or more Class II chemical preservatives are to be used in the food (e.g. sorbic acid or benzoic acid), use the Calculation Tool for Class II Chemical Preservatives to calculate the permitted levels that should be used.

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Food Additives
rules.notes: All food businesses offering food for sale in Singapore must comply with the regulatory limits on food additives.
rules.evidenceQuote: All food businesses offering food for sale in Singapore must comply with the regulatory limits on food additives.

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Food Additives
rules.notes: Food additives are allowed when there is a technological justification and they do not present health risks to consumers.
rules.evidenceQuote: Food additives are allowed when: there is a technological justification they do not present health risks to consumers

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Food Additives
rules.notes: Food additives must be assessed for safety by SFA before they can be used in food sold in Singapore.
rules.evidenceQuote: Food additives must be assessed for safety by SFA before they can be used in food sold in Singapore.

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Food Additives
rules.notes: Only food additives that have been assessed by SFA are permitted for use in food products sold in Singapore.
rules.evidenceQuote: Only food additives that have been assessed by SFA are permitted for use in food products sold in Singapore.

rules.foodCategory: seasoning powder, seasoning sauce, snack seasoning, ready-to-eat snacks
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Food Additives
rules.notes: Different limits apply depending on the additive and the type of food products. Use the List of Permitted Food Additives or Food Additives Search tool to check if additives are allowed and their corresponding limits.
rules.evidenceQuote: Download the List of Permitted Food Additives or use the Food Additives Search tool to check if your additives are allowed.

rules.foodCategory: seasoning powder, seasoning sauce, snack seasoning, ready-to-eat snacks
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Food Additives
rules.notes: If a food product contains an additive that is not permitted, it must not be imported or sold. An application can be submitted to SFA for evaluation.
rules.evidenceQuote: If your food product contains an additive that is not permitted, do not import or sell it.

rules.foodCategory: seasoning powder, seasoning sauce, snack seasoning, ready-to-eat snacks
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Food businesses must be aware of contaminants commonly found in their products and conduct laboratory tests as needed.
rules.evidenceQuote: Food businesses must be aware of the contaminants that are commonly found in their food products and conduct laboratory tests as needed.

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: All food businesses must comply with the regulatory limits on incidental constituents (contaminants) in food.
rules.evidenceQuote: All food businesses must comply with the regulatory limits on incidental constituents i.e. contaminants in food.

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Contaminants (also known as incidental constituents) in food sold in Singapore must be within SFA’s regulatory limits.
rules.evidenceQuote: Contaminants (also known as incidental constituents) in food sold in Singapore must be within SFA’s regulatory limits.

rules.foodCategory: seasoning powder, seasoning sauce, snack seasoning, ready-to-eat snacks
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: If food products contain contaminants or incidental constituents that exceed regulatory limits, they must not be imported or sold.
rules.evidenceQuote: If your food or food products contain contaminants or incidental constituents that exceed the regulatory limits, do not import or sell them.

rules.foodCategory: seasoning powder, seasoning sauce, snack seasoning, ready-to-eat snacks
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Various limits are imposed on different types of contaminants (e.g., heavy metals, mycotoxins, marine biotoxins, pesticide residues, other incidental constituents). Refer to relevant SFA documents for details.
rules.evidenceQuote: There are various limits imposed on different types of contaminants or incidental constituents in food. For more details, please refer to the relevant documents below

rules.foodCategory: meat and any food derived from meat
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Import or sale of meat and any food derived from meat containing residues of diethylstibestrol, hexoestrol, or dienoestrol is prohibited.
rules.evidenceQuote: Regulation 33 of the Food Regulations prohibits the import or sale of meat and any food derived from meat which contains residues of the following compounds: diethylstibestrol [...] hexoestrol [...] dienoestrol

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: SFA has established a list of maximum residue limits (MRLs) for other veterinary drug residues in various food commodities, which must be complied with. These MRLs can be downloaded from the SFA website.
rules.evidenceQuote: SFA has also assessed the risk of the presence of other veterinary drug residues in various food commodities, and has established a list of maximum residue limits (MRLs) for veterinary drug residues for compliance by food businesses with effect from 1 May 2020.

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Food Additives
rules.notes: All ingredients and additives used in prepacked food products must be declared in the list of ingredients on food labels.

rules.foodCategory: seasoning powder, seasoning sauce, snack seasoning, ready-to-eat snacks

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Ready-to-eat (RTE) food are not required to be tested for total plate count (TPC).
rules.evidenceQuote: Ready-to-eat (RTE) food are not required to be tested for total plate count (TPC).

rules.limit: Control measures shall be set. Precautionary labeling is suggested if cross-contamination risk exists.

rules.notes: Suggested statement: “The plant, equipment or production line used to produce the product is also used to process○○” or synonymous terms.
